- Mammogram/US: 3.1 cm left UOQ mass, BI-RADS 5; 2.0 cm left axillary node
- CT C/A/P + bone scan: No distant metastatic disease, consistent with locoregional Stage IIB

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN

38-year-old premenopausal woman with newly diagnosed Stage IIB (T2 N1 M0) invasive ductal carcinoma of the left breast, grade 3, triple-negative (ER-/PR-/HER2-), Ki-67 85%, with confirmed germline BRCA1 pathogenic variant (p.E1250*) and HRD-positive tumor. PD-L1 CPS 15 on the primary tumor.

1. NEOADJUVANT SYSTEMIC THERAPY -- PLANNED

Following multidisciplinary tumor board review, we will pursue neoadjuvant therapy rather than upfront surgery. Rationale: (a) T2N1 disease, node-positive TNBC, high-grade, high Ki-67 -- strong indication for systemic therapy; (b) neoadjuvant approach enables pathologic response assessment and may allow breast-conserving surgery; (c) PD-L1 CPS 15 makes this patient an excellent candidate for pembrolizumab-based neoadjuvant therapy per KEYNOTE-522 data.

Plan: Pembrolizumab 200 mg IV day 1 of each 21-day cycle, combined with:
  - Phase 1 (approximately 8 weeks): Dose-dense AC -- doxorubicin (Adriamycin) 60 mg/m² IV + cyclophosphamide 600 mg/m² IV every 14 days x 4 cycles (ddAC x4). Pegfilgrastim 6 mg SC on day 2 of each ddAC cycle for primary febrile neutropenia prophylaxis. Pembrolizumab cycles 1 and 3 co-administered with ddAC cycles 1 and 3 (given 3-week pembro cycle and 2-week AC cycle, timing will be coordinated by infusion nursing per schedule).
  - Phase 2 (approximately 12 weeks): Paclitaxel (Taxol) 80 mg/m² IV weekly x 12 weeks with pembrolizumab 200 mg IV every 3 weeks concurrently.

Antiemetic prophylaxis for ddAC: ondansetron 8 mg IV, dexamethasone 12 mg IV, aprepitant 125 mg PO day 1 (then 80 mg PO days 2-3). Recommended olanzapine 10 mg PO QHS days 1-4 as well given high emetogenic risk. Pre-meds for paclitaxel: diphenhydramine 25 mg IV, famotidine 20 mg IV, dexamethasone 20 mg IV.

Baseline ECHO obtained -- LVEF 63%, adequate.
Baseline LFTs normal.
Patient counseled on pembro toxicities including irAE monitoring (thyroid, liver, lung, skin, adrenal, pituitary). Immunotherapy toxicity handout provided.

2. GERMLINE BRCA1 MUTATION

Germline BRCA1 pathogenic variant p.E1250* confirmed. Referral placed to certified genetic counselor for formal pre/post-test counseling, family communication letter, and surveillance recommendations. Implications discussed with patient:
- Elevated lifetime risk for contralateral breast cancer (~40-60% cumulative risk)
- Elevated lifetime risk for ovarian cancer (~35-46% cumulative risk with BRCA1)
- Risk-reducing bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy (RRSO) recommended generally between ages 35-40 for BRCA1 carriers -- will revisit after completion of neoadjuvant treatment and surgery
- First-degree relatives (sisters, mother) should be offered cascade testing
- Implications for PARP inhibitor eligibility (olaparib) if needed in the future

3. FERTILITY PRESERVATION COUNSELING

Patient is nulliparous and premenopausal. The urgency of initiating chemotherapy and the patient's own time constraints were discussed in detail. Options reviewed: oocyte cryopreservation (egg freezing), embryo cryopreservation. Referral offered to reproductive endocrinology; patient declined at this time citing time constraints and the desire to start treatment promptly. This decision was documented and respected. Patient counseled that chemotherapy-induced premature ovarian insufficiency is a risk with this regimen. We will monitor FSH/LH/estradiol and AMH pre- and post-treatment. Patient was provided with written information and may revisit fertility options if she changes her mind.

4. LABS AND MONITORING

Pre-treatment baseline: CBC, CMP, LFTs, TSH, free T4, pregnancy test (urine hCG -- patient advised to use non-hormonal contraception during treatment), hepatitis B sAg and sAb (HBSAB) prior to pembrolizumab initiation, ECHO (completed).
Monitoring during treatment: CBC and CMP with each ddAC cycle. LFTs, TSH, free T4 each pembrolizumab cycle. EKG not required at baseline given normal ECHO.

5. SUPPORTIVE CARE

- Social work referral placed (psychosocial support, financial assistance resources)
- Oncology dietitian referral offered
- Patient given Infusion center orientation appointment
- Discussed alopecia as expected side effect with doxorubicin-based therapy; cold cap therapy discussed but not offered with this regimen given tumor biology and concern for scalp metastasis prophylaxis
- Patient encouraged to remain active as tolerated

6. PLAN / FOLLOW-UP

- Start pembrolizumab + ddAC cycle 1 within the next 7-10 days once pre-treatment work-up complete
- Follow up in clinic on day 1 of each cycle for toxicity assessment
- Mid-treatment response assessment MRI of left breast/axilla planned after completion of ddAC x4 (prior to starting weekly Taxol phase)
- Surgical oncology to remain engaged for post-neoadjuvant surgical planning

Extensive time was spent with Ms. Okonkwo and her partner today -- approximately 75 minutes. All questions addressed. She understands the diagnosis, treatment rationale, and goals of therapy. She is motivated and engaged in her care.
